title: Rule model and config loader (.jit/rules.toml)
description:
Define the rule data model and the `.jit/rules.toml` loader, including selector matching, the raw-schema-file rule, and the shorthand-vs-file guard.

## Background

A rule is selector + assertion + severity + enforce. The loader parses rules.toml and computes the union of applicable rules for an issue. Raw JSON Schema lives only in files (TOML cannot faithfully express schemas). See `dev/active/f6a704d0-validation-engine-plan.md` ("The Rule model") and decision record sections 3, 4, 7.2, 8.1.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] [hard] `validation/rules.rs` defines `Rule { name, when: Selector, severity, enforce, assert, scope }`, `Selector` over `type`/`label`/`state`/`has_doc_type`, and `Severity { off, warn, error }`.
- [ ] [hard] `.jit/rules.toml` parses into `Vec<Rule>`; an absent `enforce` defaults to false.
- [ ] [hard] Selector matching is AND across present dimensions; `matching_rules(issue)` returns the union; `label` selectors support `ns:*` wildcards; matching reads only cheap `Issue` fields (no description parse).
- [ ] [hard] A raw `json-schema` assertion MUST be a `.jit/schemas/<name>.json` file reference; inline raw JSON Schema in TOML is rejected with a clear config error. Inline TOML holds only shorthand kinds.
- [ ] [hard] A rule is shorthand XOR file-schema; combining them in one rule is a config error.
- [ ] [hard] Regex-bearing shorthand fields (`label-value-pattern`) authored as TOML literal strings (`'...'`) round-trip to `serde_json` intact, verified by a unit test.
- [ ] [hard] The parsed ruleset/config is cached on the executor (parsed once per process, not re-read per write).

## Notes

Anchors: config load in `config_manager.rs:51-53` / `config.rs:405-430`; `toml` 0.8. Conversion uses serde transcoding (`serde_json::to_value`) for file schemas; inline shorthands deserialize directly.
